polls.text: Are there any objections to the WG working on templates?
Is this document a suitable starting point for the WG to base it's work?
Is there interest in working on this problem in the WG?
Is this document a suitable starting point for the WG to base it's work?

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text: Don't forget to say your name at the mic please :blush:

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: For me examples are very helpful both as a reviewer and as an author. Specially if it validates the model/prose in the draft. The size does not bother me.

chat.author: Maria Matějka
chat.text: Examples are a must, they confirm what happens in the text and they should get reviewed thoroughly together with the text.

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: I don't think I have ever encountered a document with too many examples. It's always too few.

chat.author: Lou Berger
chat.text: As long as they are correct...

chat.author: Lou Berger
chat.text: https://github.com/netmod-wg/yang-next/issues

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text:
Please check the notes as we go to ensure I captured your name and comments correctly - Thanks all!
https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-netmod?both

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: Examples should be correct because they are generated by the tooling (rather than by hand).

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: Sorry, I keep forgetting to say my name at the Mic.

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: I second Maria's comment, there are discrepancies between YANG-XML, YANG-JSON and YANG-CBOR and this has been an issue for some YANG-related work.

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: I expect examples in documents to have automated tests which are executed during doc build

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: @reshad would transaction-id help in your use case?

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: @Per with what James and Rob explained, I think I'm good with <running>

chat.author: Maria Matějka
chat.text: we'll look into the templating thing in BIRD, as it may be a good idea to align the concepts so that we could represent our config templates with YANG templates

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: +1 on trying to align the YANG-Next solution and the 1.1 solution in this document

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: I do wonder why the words "absolute" and "relative" are needed here.  Wouldn't the leading '/' be enough to show absolute?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Isn't this DISPLAY-HINTS from MIBs?

chat.author: Kent Watsen
chat.text: @meetecho please point camera at presenter

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text:
I should say, similar.  Instead of a MAC encoded as hex bytes, DISPLAY-HINT would display it as a colon separated list.
In this case, you want to take a string and have a generic statement that says how to treat that string.

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: Thanks a lot @Vivek for this report. This is great to show this comparison within NETMOD.

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: Can you rerun all the tests on your laptop to warm up the room? :p
